icpData.name: Super Mom — Money Angle
icpData.demographics: Female · 28–44 · married · 2–3 kids · HHI $40–85K · suburban · primary grocery buyer · budget-tracker
icpData.psychographics: Financially aware. Responds to dollar figures before emotional scenes. Loss aversion is financial first. Will calculate ROI before she feels the feeling.
icpData.primary_pain: The invisible leak — $111/month in groceries that expire before they become dinner
icpData.secondary_pain: 6:30 PM decision fatigue — real but secondary. She would fix the money first.
icpData.value_trigger: $1,336 back without couponing. $7.99/month to save $111/month. She sees it in one line.
icpData.loss_aversion: Every dollar in the bin is a dollar her family does not have. Every day she waits costs $3.66.
icpData.message_hierarchy: 1.$1,336/year · 2.$111/month · 3.30 min from what you have · 4.one app closes the loop · 5.built by first responders
icpData.conversion_triggers: $1,336 figure · $7.99 to save $111 ROI · 10,000 households validated · 7-day free trial

themeData.emotional_entry: recognition — she knows the leak exists, she just has not named it
themeData.emotional_payoff: relief — the leak is closed in dollars and evenings
themeData.hook_angle: You have an invisible leak. It costs $111 a month. Five apps were never going to fix it.
themeData.problem_angle: Five apps. None of them talk to each other. The leak runs in the gap between them.
themeData.agitate_angle: The spinach. The ground beef. The yogurt. $111 gone. Every month. No system.
themeData.food_type: whatever is already in your fridge — expiring produce · ground beef · yogurt · the groceries that never became dinner
themeData.feature_proof: $1,336/year average savings · 69.5% less food waste · 30 minutes fridge to table · validated across 10,000 household profiles
